core_mechanism: a systematic perturbative (asymptotic) expansion of observables in powers of a single small dimensionless parameter, organized via an effective-field-theory scale separation so that only a few low-energy effective parameters enter at leading order and higher-order corrections bring in further microscopic details
brief_reason: The abstract builds a systematic perturbative framework using effective field theory whose expansion is 'essentially an expansion in the gas parameter sqrt(na^3)', with leading corrections set by the scattering length and nonuniversal higher-order corrections depending on additional potential details such as the effective range.
